component.name: UnifiedFloppyTool
component.summary: Forensic floppy disk preservation for archives and museums

description.p: UnifiedFloppyTool (UFT) is a Qt 6 desktop application for the forensic capture, analysis and long-term preservation of historical floppy disks. It targets archives, museums, digital forensics labs and serious retrocomputing enthusiasts who need the full truth about the magnetic layer — not just a working copy.

ul.li: Reads and writes 80+ disk image formats across Commodore, Apple, Atari, IBM PC, Amstrad, Spectrum, BBC/Acorn, Amiga, and Japanese 8-bit/16-bit systems (SCP, HFE, IPF, WOZ, ADF, D64, IMD, TD0 and many more)
Drives six hardware controllers (Greaseweazle, SuperCard Pro, KryoFlux, FC5025, XUM1541/ZoomFloppy, Applesauce)
DeepRead signal recovery pipeline (OTDR-inspired flux analysis, adaptive PLL re-decode, multi-revolution weighted voting)
Detects and documents 35+ historical copy-protection schemes
Forensic report with hash chain, audit trail and sidecar metadata that record every transformation in a lossy-documented format

description.p: UFT follows a strict preservation philosophy: no silent data loss, no fabricated flux, no hidden normalisations. Every conversion is categorised (lossless, lossy-documented, impossible) and destructive operations require explicit consent.

screenshot.caption: Main window showing the OTDR-based flux analysis panel

description.p: Design-principles release. Adds forensic-integrity infrastructure: loss-report sidecar (.loss.json) schema v1, explicit round-trip matrix with LL/LD/IM/UT statuses, preflight check for every conversion, plugin metadata for spec-status / feature-matrix / emulator-compatibility / stub-marker, GCC 15 (C23) build fix.
First tagged release with the full 80-plugin format catalogue and 6 hardware controllers. CI green on Linux (Qt 6.7/6.10), macOS and Windows MinGW.
